topic: Argument hierarchy — are the top kill-shots actually our strongest arguments?
scope: Phase 2 rerun (commissioned 2026-05-28). Re-evaluates argument hierarchy after the V6 restructure (2026-04-07) and ~7 weeks of substantive content changes since the original c1 pass (2026-04-07). Original c1 review found 9 holes about kill-shot ordering, self-contradiction promotion, and frame-of-reference vulnerability of external-data arguments. V6 restructure substantially addressed the structural recommendations (self-contradictions promoted to Part 2 with explicit "Why these come first" framing; Part 5 reframed as dome-binary-tests not our-top-3; Aetheric Refraction promoted to Part 1.5). This c2 pass evaluates what new hierarchy issues have emerged since.
our_argument_summary: The review currently uses a 3-tier framework documented in Part 1 "How This Review Is Organized": Tier 1 = self-contradictions (Parts 2, 2b); Tier 2 = per-WIN evaluation (Part 3); Tier 3 = kill-shots + falsification (Parts 5, 7). Part 2 leads with the framing "Why these arguments come first: internal consistency cannot be deflected by frame-of-reference objections." Part 9.1 Pattern 11 explicitly calls self-contradictions "the strongest argument against the model." The structural promotion recommended by c1 has substantially landed. However, sub-hierarchy and out-of-scope-placement issues remain.

prior_cycle_status.holes_substantially_addressed: c1 Hole 1 (kill-shot order favoring external falsification) — RESOLVED by V6 restructure: Part 2 now leads, Part 7 follows. c1 Hole 2 (3 external kill-shots vulnerable to frame-of-reference objection) — RESOLVED in framing: Part 2 intro explicitly says "External data can always be disputed (your instruments assume the globe)." c1 Hole 3 (sun/firmament collision underdeveloped) — PARTIALLY: section 1.5 now houses the collision argument under Aetheric Refraction. c1 Hole 4 (Schumann not in headline kill-shots) — RESOLVED: 2.1 Schumann is the lead self-contradiction. c1 Holes 5-9 (tidal-five-WIN consolidation, satellite-altitude language, Tier 1 / Tier 2 split, Aetheric Refraction placement, equal-strength-implied kill-shots) — all visible as RESOLVED or substantially mitigated by V6.
prior_cycle_status.new_holes_emerged_since_v6: Yes — the Part 2 internal ordering, summary placement, and absent-from-Part-2 strong-SC WINs are all new issues that did not exist when Part 2 was created.

holes_found.description: Part 1.5-1.9 house Tier-1-class structural attacks — Aetheric Refraction critique (1.5), Dielectric Foundation (1.6), Gravity Paradox (1.7), Coordinate Collapse (1.8), Differential Refraction (1.9) — but they live inside the "What Is the Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model?" tab and the explicit Tier 1/2/3 signposting in "How This Review Is Organized" classifies only Parts 2 and 2b as Tier 1. Coordinate Collapse (1.8) in particular is a single-shot whole-model kill: the dome's refraction fix invalidates its own Polaris-elevation-derived coordinate system because Polaris light travels through the same n(r) medium (medium 300-6,000x more refractive than Earth's atmosphere). A reader who internalizes the tier framework expects the strongest arguments in Parts 2 and 2b. A defender skim-reading only the model-description tab encounters these critiques as if they were exposition asides — easy to dismiss as opinion. Aetheric Refraction (1.5) and Coordinate Collapse (1.8) use dome-parameters-only logic and are at least as strong as some Part 2 subsections (e.g., 2.6 Polaris distance, 2.7 Eclipse duration).
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) update "How This Review Is Organized" to enumerate the in-Part-1 structural attacks as Tier 1 alongside Parts 2 and 2b — e.g., "Tier 1: Parts 1.5, 1.7, 1.8, 1.9 (structural attacks on the model's framework) + Parts 2 and 2b (per-prediction self-contradictions and code analysis)"; OR (b) move 1.5-1.9 into a new Part 1c "Structural Attacks on the Framework" placed between Part 1 (model description) and Part 2 (self-contradictions), keeping the tier system clean. Option (a) is lower-cost; option (b) is structurally cleaner. The current state is the worst of both — the attacks are written but tier-orphaned.

holes_found.description: Part 2 internal ordering: 2.9 "The V13 Coordinate System: A Self-Referential Loop" is the foundation argument (the entire coordinate system that produces every other dome distance is internally inconsistent with the V13 refraction fix), yet placed last in Part 2 before the Summary. A reader who exits at 2.5 sees five narrow per-prediction SCs (Schumann, tidal, gravity, solar diameter, star positions) but never the structural foundation that makes them all unavoidable. Within Part 2 the subsections currently signal no strength ranking — 2.1 Schumann is first by topic alphabet-ish, not by strongest-first. The Tier 1 framing in Part 1 says self-contradictions are the strongest argument class, but within Tier 1 there is no further ranking guidance.
holes_found.recommendation: Promote 2.9 V13 Coordinate System to 2.1 or 2.2 — it is the structural argument that frames why every other per-prediction SC is fatal. Alternative: add a one-line strength annotation at the top of each Part 2 subsection (e.g., "Strongest possible: this SC requires no external observation" vs "Confirms the pattern but requires Polaris elevation data"). The current ordering inverts strength; either reorder or annotate.

holes_found.description: Part 2 hierarchy bug: the "Summary: The Model Refutes Itself" header sits at char ~39,516 of a 58,717-char section, but THREE substantive arguments follow it — Axial Symmetry Paradox (~41,239), Sign-Only Predictions (~45,775), and the V51.0 Cosmological Expansion / cosmological-cluster consolidation (~52,887). The Summary's own prose claims "Twelve independent derivations" and includes the loose three in that count, but the placement signals "section concluded" to readers. A reader who exits at the Summary misses ~13 KB of substantive arguments including the consolidated cosmological-scale cluster (WIN-047 Hubble Law, WIN-048 CMB Axis of Evil, WIN-052 RAR, WIN-054 El Gordo, WIN-055 Cepheid/SBF distance-redshift) — five Self-Contradicted WINs that share one fatal structural objection (no galaxy-scale physics framework). The Axial Symmetry Paradox is similarly orphaned: it is the foundational "H(r) has no longitude variable yet model predicts longitude-dependent phenomena" argument that ties together at least 12 named WINs (per its own prose), but appears as an unnumbered post-summary aside.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) move the Summary to true end-of-Part-2 (after the V51.0 Cosmological Expansion section), so the reader-completion signal aligns with section-end; OR (b) renumber the three loose arguments as 2.10 Axial Symmetry Paradox, 2.11 Sign-Only Predictions, 2.12 Cosmological-Scale Cluster, making them part of the visible structure before the Summary. Option (b) also fixes Hole HIER-PHASE2-04 (absent strong SCs) by giving the cosmological cluster a numbered home.

holes_found.description: Twenty WINs are tagged verdict=Self-Contradicted in data/wins.json (data/wins.json verdict count, 2026-05-29 snapshot: SC=20). Part 2 features 9 named subsections (2.1-2.9) + 3 loose post-summary arguments = 12 derivations. Eight strong SCs are referenced only via the Part 3 per-WIN table and never surface in Part 2's structural argument arc. Particularly egregious omissions: WIN-012 (κ = 1.67 nT/µGal coupling derived from BOU 2017 eclipse / Mohe 1997 eclipse; WIN-013 and WIN-014 then report 0.0 µGal at later eclipses — κ is a divide-by-zero with the dome's own subsequent data, AND κ is the keystone of the dome's August 2026 marquee eclipse prediction); WIN-053 (dome's ring-magnet geometry predicts B_south ≈ 39 nT via flux conservation; the dome's fitted equation gives 64,852 nT — a 1,660:1 self-contradiction of dome geometry vs dome curve-fit); WIN-044 (Firmament Scaling Function computes to 1.47 under V51 parameters but 1.89 under V50.6 — version-unstable geometry, the same formula produces different numbers as parameters drift). These are arguably stronger than 2.6 Polaris distance and 2.7 Eclipse duration (both of which have rebuttable details) yet they are invisible in the Part 2 structural narrative.
holes_found.recommendation: Add 2-3 new Part 2 subsections featuring the strongest absent SCs. Suggested: 2.10 "κ Divide-by-Zero: the BOU/Mohe Coupling vs Later Eclipse Data" (WIN-012/013/014 cluster — high impact because κ also drives the dome's August 2026 eclipse marquee); 2.11 "1,660:1 Ring-Magnet vs Fitted B(r) Mismatch" (WIN-053 — devastating because the dome's own ring-magnet topology gives a number 1,660x off its own fitted equation); 2.12 "Version-Unstable Geometry: FSF V50.6 → V51 Shift" (WIN-044 — clean attack because the same formula gives different answers as parameters drift, exposing curve-fit-not-derivation). These can be added without rewriting the cited WINs — Part 2 subsection prose can summarize and link to the per-WIN detail.

holes_found.description: The 3-tier signposting in "How This Review Is Organized" sits inside Part 1 (model description tab), at the top of tab 1. A reader on any other tab (selftest, wins, pages, killshots, predictions, falsify, ai, refs) does not encounter this tier framework unless they specifically backtrack. Tab-direct entry from search engines is common — a Google snippet pointing at Part 5 (Kill-Shot Binary Tests) takes a reader who never sees the tier guidance. The label "Kill-Shot" on Part 5 is dome-author-defined; the actual Tier-1 strongest arguments live in Part 2 under "Self-Contradictions." A search-driven reader landing on Part 5 may form a hierarchy impression backwards.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a compact 2-3 sentence "How This Review Is Organized" callout box at the top of Parts 2, 5, and 7 — the three tabs most likely to be entered directly. The callout should say: "This review is organized by argument strength: Tier 1 (self-contradictions in Parts 2 and 2b) is the strongest; Tier 2 (Part 3 per-WIN review) is supporting; Tier 3 (this section, plus Parts 5/7) is independent confirmation." Lightweight, repeats the framing without duplicating Part 1 content.

holes_found.description: "Kill-Shot" label collision. Part 5 is named "Kill-Shot Binary Tests" — these are the DOME's own binary tests rebutted, not our strongest arguments. The Tier 3 grouping in "How This Review Is Organized" puts Parts 5 and 7 together ("kill-shot binary tests and falsification tests"). A reader searching the site for the review's strongest attacks may navigate by name to Part 5 expecting "our top kill-shots" but find dome-author-defined tests instead. The actual Tier-1 arguments live in Part 2 under the un-marketable name "Self-Contradictions." The naming mismatch is a real reader-path attack surface: a fair-minded skim reader who sees "Kill-Shot Binary Tests" as a tab assumes that's our strongest tab.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) rename Part 5 from "Kill-Shot Binary Tests" to "Refuting the Dome's Binary Tests" to disambiguate that the tests are the dome's framework; OR (b) rename Part 2 from "Self-Contradictions" to "Self-Contradictions: The Review's Strongest Case" to put the strength signal in the nav label. Option (a) is lower-cost (Part 5 is the smaller of the two). Operator preference; flagging the disambiguation problem.

holes_found.description: Within-Part-2 strength ranking absent. Subsections 2.1-2.9 are presented with no indication of which is the strongest. A defender exiting at 2.1 (Schumann) has no way to know that 2.9 (V13 coordinate self-referential loop) is the foundation argument. Even after Hole HIER-PHASE2-02 reorders, an explicit strength annotation would help a skim reader. Operator may choose to address this jointly with HIER-PHASE2-02 or independently.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional but low-cost: add a single small italic line at the top of each 2.x subsection: "This is a topic-narrow SC; the more foundational structural argument is at 2.X." OR add the strongest-3 tag (e.g., a unicode marker like ★) next to subsection numbers. This is belt-and-braces over HIER-PHASE2-02.

stronger_arguments.description: The cosmological-scale cluster — 5 SC WINs (047 Hubble Law, 048 CMB Axis of Evil, 052 RAR, 054 El Gordo, 055 Cepheid/SBF) — shares ONE underlying structural failure: the dome has bounded firmament geometry (H(r) maxes at 8,537 km at the pole) yet claims to predict observables from galaxies at megaparsec distances. There is no aetheric framework that scales from disc-radius geometry to galaxy-scale observables. This is a single, devastating attack that is currently fragmented across 5 WINs and a buried post-summary paragraph ("The V51.0 Cosmological Expansion: Territory Without a Map"). Lifting this to a numbered Part 2 subsection (e.g., 2.12 per HIER-PHASE2-03 / 04) would consolidate the case.

WIN-012 κ = 1.67 nT/µGal vs WIN-013/014 reporting 0.0 µGal at later eclipses is a divide-by-zero in the dome's OWN parameter chain — κ is also the coupling that drives the August 2026 marquee eclipse prediction. This is currently absent from Part 2 as a named structural subsection even though it is arguably the single most important coupling-constant attack in the entire review (the marquee prediction depends on a coefficient that the dome's own later data nullifies).

kernel_of_truth.description: The V6 restructure (2026-04-07) substantially adopted the original c1 review's top recommendation: self-contradictions are now the FIRST argument class the reader encounters after the model description, with explicit "Why these arguments come first" framing in Part 2's intro paragraph. The 3-tier signposting in Part 1 ("How This Review Is Organized") is a real improvement over the pre-V6 "survey-the-landscape" structure. The author of the V6 restructure correctly identified that internal consistency arguments cannot be deflected by frame-of-reference objections, and structurally promoted that argument class. The remaining hierarchy issues in this c2 review are sub-hierarchy issues — placement within Part 2 (Hole 02-03), absent-strong-SCs in Part 2 (Hole 04), and tier-system-omitted attacks in Part 1.5-1.9 (Hole 01). The macrostructure is good. (Per HOLISTIC-HOL-HIERARCHY.json c1 finding 1 and the V6 restructure note in CLAUDE.md.)
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Macrostructure good is not the same as hierarchy correct. A reader who internalizes the tier framework expects ALL Tier-1 arguments in Parts 2 and 2b, but Part 1.5/1.7/1.8/1.9 contain Tier-1-class attacks that the tier system does not advertise (Hole 01). Within Part 2, the foundation argument (V13 coordinate self-referential loop at 2.9) is at the bottom and the post-summary loose arguments are hierarchy-orphaned (Hole 02-03). Strong SCs are absent from the structural argument arc despite being verdict-tagged SC in wins.json (Hole 04). The V6 macrostructure correctly identified what mattered — promote SCs — but did not finish the job within the SC section itself or in the model-description tab.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender who has read this review's own methodological principles (internal consistency first, etc.) could respond: "You're critiquing pacing of a slam-dunk. You have 20 self-contradictions in your own count. The fact that you keep finding more arguments to add to Part 2 just confirms the case is overdetermined. Your reader-exit-at-the-Summary critique assumes readers who care will quit early — but readers who care DO read past summaries. The Coordinate Collapse argument lives in Part 1 because it IS part of describing what the model is — you cannot describe the model without describing why its coordinate system fails. Your Kill-Shot label-collision critique assumes search-driven readers — but search-driven readers will read Part 5's intro and learn that these are the DOME's tests recast. You are nitpicking pacing of a 100-0 victory. Spend your effort on new attack surface, not on rearranging deck chairs."
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add a 2-sentence acknowledgement in Part 1.1 Overview: "The case below is overdetermined — any one of the 20 self-contradictions in Part 2 alone falsifies the model. The argument hierarchy is for navigation, not necessity." This preempts the defender's "you're nitpicking a slam-dunk" rebuttal by acknowledging it up-front, and reframes the hierarchy as a usability concern, not a necessity-of-argument concern. The HIER-PHASE2-02 (V13 ordering), HIER-PHASE2-03 (Summary placement), and HIER-PHASE2-04 (absent strong SCs) recommendations remain valid because they DO improve persuasion at the margin — defender concession that "readers who care will read past summaries" admits the marginal reader is the one whose path needs fixing.

claims_checked: Self-Contradicted WIN count: 20 (counted from data/wins.json verdict field)
Three post-Summary loose arguments at offsets 41,239 / 45,775 / 52,887 (indexOf scan)
quantitative_verification.computations: Used node.js indexOf scans on sections.json:part2.html for header offsets. Used wins.json verdict-field count. All numbers reproducible by re-running the scan commands in this review session log.

deeper_analysis:
The c1 review (2026-04-07) found 9 holes; the V6 restructure substantially addressed the structural top-3 (self-contradictions promoted, Schumann featured, frame-of-reference vulnerability framed). The Phase 2 commission asked: what hierarchy issues have emerged since? Answer: sub-hierarchy issues internal to Part 2, plus a tier-system blind spot in Part 1.5-1.9.

The most actionable single change is renumbering the three post-Summary loose arguments as 2.10/2.11/2.12 and moving the Summary to true-end-of-Part-2. This fixes HIER-PHASE2-03 (Summary placement) AND partially fixes HIER-PHASE2-04 (absent strong SCs, because the cosmological cluster gets a number). It is a single sections.json edit.

The most consequential change is HIER-PHASE2-04 (add 2-3 new subsections for WIN-012/053/044). This adds the κ divide-by-zero argument — which is also the keystone of the dome's marquee eclipse prediction — to the Part 2 structural arc where it belongs.

The most contested change is HIER-PHASE2-06 (Part 5 vs Part 2 naming). The advocate-mode rebuttal (defender concedes readers who care will read intros) partially defends current naming. Operator may defer this.

All 7 findings are surfaced as recommendations, not as required changes. The current state is functional. The Phase 2 ask was to find what could be improved after the V6 macrostructure landed — and the answer is: internal Part 2 polish (Holes 02-04), tier-system completeness (Hole 01), and tab-entry callout (Hole 05). Holes 06-07 are lower-priority cosmetic.

citations_verified: data/wins.json verdict counts (verified by direct scan)
Part 1 "How This Review Is Organized" tier framing (verified by direct read)
CLAUDE.md V6 restructure note (verified by direct read from project CLAUDE.md)
citations_unchecked: Whether the V51.0 Cosmological Expansion section's cosmological-cluster anchor target appears anywhere else in the site (not verified — only confirmed it exists at offset ~52,887 in part2.html with id="cosmological-cluster")

summary_for_decider: HOL-HIERARCHY Phase 2 rerun finds 4 major + 2 moderate + 1 minor holes. All 7 are sub-hierarchy issues internal to Part 2 and Part 1.5-1.9; the V6 macrostructure addressed the c1 top-3 cleanly. Recommended actions in priority order: (1) HIER-PHASE2-03 + 04 joint fix: renumber Axial Symmetry / Sign-Only / Cosmological-Scale Cluster as 2.10-2.12 AND add 2.13 κ-divide-by-zero (WIN-012/013/014), 2.14 1,660:1 ring-magnet mismatch (WIN-053), 2.15 FSF version-unstable (WIN-044). Single coordinated EXP. (2) HIER-PHASE2-02: promote V13 coordinate system from 2.9 to 2.1 or 2.2. (3) HIER-PHASE2-01: update "How This Review Is Organized" tier classification to include 1.5/1.7/1.8/1.9 as Tier 1. (4) HIER-PHASE2-05: callout box at top of Parts 2/5/7. (5) HIER-PHASE2-06 (kill-shot label) and HIER-PHASE2-07 (within-Part-2 ranking) are lower-priority cosmetic. Advocate mode finds the operator may want to add a 2-sentence "case is overdetermined" acknowledgement to Part 1.1 to preempt the "you're nitpicking a slam-dunk" rebuttal. NOT a queue-sourced review — no queue_id field.
